weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Strength + power: heavy compound work paired with jumps, throws, and short sprints. Everything is fresh this week — read the cues, rest fully before each explosive effort, and keep it to 3 sets. Progress with load or sharper speed, never an added set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2–3 min between sets · max-quality, fast stand off the box
patterns.load: A KB 2 sizes HEAVIER than competition, at competition box height
cues: Treat all 5 reps as your best, fastest standups of the day.
Pause briefly on the box, stay tight, then drive straight up.
Rest the full 2–3 minutes so power stays high every set.
Keep your core braced — stop the set the moment your back rounds.

patterns.name: Seated Box to Broad Jump
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · sit to the box, then jump forward for distance
patterns.load: Bodyweight only — explosive, no added load
cues: Sit fully to the box, then swing your arms and jump forward off the floor.
Land soft on two feet with bent knees and stick the landing.
Stand up and walk back to reset fully before each jump.
Quality over distance — a clean controlled landing beats an extra inch.

patterns.name: KB Heartbeat Squat Pulse
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · short fast pulse out of the bottom of each squat
patterns.load: A KB about 1 size heavier than competition, held at the chest
cues: Squat down, then snap up out of the bottom with a quick punchy drive.
Stand fully tall at the top of every rep before the next one.
Keep the bell locked to your chest so the speed comes from your legs.
Reset your brace each rep — fast does not mean loose.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 throws · rest 2 min between sets · 9 throws, every one a max-quality effort
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Drop into a low hinge and explode up, launching the ball up and back over your head for maximum distance.
Every throw is a full-power rep — chase your farthest throw, then fully reset.
Power comes from snapping the hips open fast; keep the arms loose and let them whip the ball over.
Take long rest between sets so the ball moves fast every single attempt.

patterns.name: Low Box Step-Up Jump
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps each leg · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight onto a low, stable box (knee height or lower)
cues: Plant one foot on a low box and drive up explosively until your driving leg is tall, then step back down with control.
Push hard through the box-leg heel and reach tall — this builds the explosive hip drive your throw needs.
Use a low box and step down softly one foot at a time — do not jump or hop off the box.
Hold a rail or sturdy support nearby if you want extra balance security.

patterns.name: Bent-Over DB Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A pair of DBs heavy enough that rep 8 is a real effort with a flat back
cues: Hinge forward with a flat back, let the DBs hang, then row them to your ribs by driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top and lower under control — this strengthens the back that decelerates the ball.
Keep your back flat and your neck long; don't round or jerk the weight up with your back.
Brace your stomach the whole set to protect your lower back.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~55% of your max-rep test · rest 2 min between · explosive press, controlled lower
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — lower under control, then drive the bar up off your chest as fast as it will move.
Treat every rep as a max-quality effort and take the full 2-minute rest so the bar stays fast.
End the set when bar speed drops or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Banded Speed Bench Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · explosive press, controlled lower
patterns.load: An empty or light bar with a band looped over it for speed — light enough that the bar flies up fast for all 6 reps
cues: Lower the bar under control to your chest, then press it up as fast as it will move against the band's pull.
Keep both feet planted and your hips down on the bench the whole set — the speed comes from the press, not a bounce.
Re-rack the bar safely onto the rack hooks between reps if you need to reset; never lower a loaded bar toward your face.
Stop the set the moment the bar slows down — this drill is only useful while every rep is fast.

patterns.name: Spoto Press with DBs
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: Two DBs heavier than your usual press DBs — challenging for 6 reps
cues: Press two DBs up, lower them to about an inch above your chest, pause for one count, then drive them back up fast.
Holding that pause an inch off the chest builds power right where the comp bench is hardest.
Keep the DBs steady over your chest during the pause. Stop the set if you can't hold the pause cleanly.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 3 min between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — full rest, treat each hold as max-quality and step down at 80%
cues: Pack the shoulders and stay rock-steady; no swinging or kipping.
Crush the bar with spread fingers and breathe slow and even.
Step down on a strong note rather than dropping when grip fails.
Write down the hold time for the app to track.

patterns.name: Fat-Grip Pull-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min 30 sec between sets
patterns.load: Fat grips on the pull-up bar · band-assist under one foot if 5 clean reps is too many
cues: The thick grip overloads the hand — pull explosively to your chin each rep.
Drive your elbows down and pinch the shoulder blades at the top.
Lower under control to a full hang; do not just drop off the bar.
Loop a band under one foot for assistance if you cannot reach 5 strict.

patterns.name: Plate Pinch Hold
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: Two smooth 10–25 lb plates pinched together in each hand — pick a weight your fingers can hold 15–30 seconds
cues: Pinch the plates with your thumb on one side and fingers on the other — no hook.
Stand tall and hold them at your sides until your fingers give out.
Set the plates down safely the instant they start to slip — that is your time.
This trains the explosive thumb-and-finger crush behind a strong hang.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball, one size heavier
cues: Fewer reps, max intent — treat every slam like a throw for maximum speed.
Full overhead reach, then drive the ball down hard and reset fresh each rep.
Rest the full two minutes so every set stays explosive.

patterns.name: Countermovement Jump to Stick
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight only — no box, jump straight up and land in the same spot
cues: Dip fast, throw your arms up, and jump straight up as high as you can off the floor — no box, nothing to clear.
Land in the same spot on the balls of both feet, bend your knees to absorb, and stick it quiet and balanced.
Reset fully and stand tall before each rep — this explosive jump trains the same fast hip drive your slam needs.
Hold a rail nearby for steadiness if you want it; never reach for height you can't land softly.

patterns.name: D-Ball Push Press from Floor
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A heavy D-ball, 15 lb (women) / 25 lb (men)
cues: Pick the ball off the floor to your chest, dip your knees, then drive it overhead with your legs.
The pop comes from a sharp leg drive, not a slow arm press.
Lower to your chest and reset on the floor before the next rep.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · rest 2 min between sets · fast turnover, count your reps
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at a fast comp-plus pace
cues: Drive a quick rope and quick feet, and note your singles each set.
Keep your jumps barely off the floor so you can match the fast rope.
Rest the full two minutes so every set is sharp and fast.

patterns.name: Explosive Single-Leg Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps each leg · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight plus a 15–25 lb DB held on the working side; rail for balance
cues: Drive up onto the ball of your foot as fast as you can on every rep.
Lower under control for a slow count to load the calf safely.
Use the rail for balance only — let the working leg supply all the power.
Pick a DB in the 15–25 lb range that lets you still drive up fast and finish all 10 with good height.

patterns.name: Continuous Speed Pogos
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 sec of fast pogos · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, fast ground contacts
cues: Bounce as fast as you can with stiff ankles, spending as little time on the floor as possible.
Keep the bounces low and quick rather than high and slow.
Stay tall and let the ankles snap — don't sink into a squat.

patterns.name: Standing Vertical Jump to Stick
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · jump high, land soft
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Dip fast, throw the arms up, and jump straight up as high as you can, landing in the same spot.
Land on the balls of your feet and bend the knees to absorb — quiet, controlled landings every time.
Reset fully between reps. Treat each jump as a max-quality effort, not a rushed bounce.

patterns.name: Single-Leg Box Step-Down
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps each leg · rest 90 sec between sets · 3-sec lower
patterns.load: Bodyweight on a knee-height box; hold a light DB at your chest if you want more challenge
cues: Stand on the box on one leg and lower the other foot slowly toward the floor under full control.
Keep the working knee tracking over your toes and your hips level — don't let one hip drop.
Tap the floor lightly, then drive back up through the standing heel. Hold a rail for balance as needed.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · powerful and crisp
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Load the arms back, explode the hips forward, and jump for distance — every rep a max-quality effort.
Stick the landing on both feet, absorbing softly through the knees and hips so nothing jars.
Walk back and reset completely between jumps. End the set if a landing gets sloppy.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 300m · rest 2–3 min fully between · faster than your 500m race pace
patterns.load: Rower · about 2–3 sec/500m FASTER than your goal 500m split, full recovery between
cues: Open with two or three full-power strokes, then hold the fast split to the end of each piece.
Sit tall and snap the legs down hard — let the legs do the work, not just the arms.
Rest fully between pieces so each 300m is a max-quality effort.

patterns.name: DB Power High-Pull
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Two DBs light enough to move explosively for 8 — about half your usual row weight
cues: Stand tall, dip slightly, then drive the DBs upward fast until your elbows reach shoulder height.
Lead with the elbows and keep the DBs close to your body on the way up.
Lower under control and reset each rep — this is about speed up, not heavy weight.

patterns.name: Box Step-Up Drive
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps each leg · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight onto a knee-high box, or hold one light DB · explosive drive up
cues: Plant the whole foot on the box, then drive up powerfully through the heel to stand tall.
Step back down under control — don't bounce or jump down off the box.
Hold a rail for balance if you need it; the focus is a fast, strong drive up.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 100-yd shuttle (2 × 50 yd) · rest 2–3 min · fast with sharp turns
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run these fast — at or just under your comp race pace
cues: Run down 50 and back 50 fast, treating each as a max-quality effort.
Decelerate hard but controlled into the single turn, plant, and re-accelerate explosively.
Rest fully so all three short shuttles stay sharp and quick.

patterns.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · firm controlled pulls · rest 2–3 min
patterns.load: Trap bar (or two dumbbells), ~70–80% of a load you could lift once — heavy, clean form
cues: Set your hips, brace your trunk, and stand the weight up by driving your feet through the floor.
Keep your back flat and arms straight — push the floor away rather than yanking with your back.
Heavy and low-rep this week; reset your setup before every pull.

patterns.name: Standing Long Jump
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 jumps · stick each landing · rest 90 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Swing your arms, load your hips, and jump forward as far as you can, landing soft and balanced.
Bend your knees to absorb the landing and hold it for a beat — quality and control over distance.
These explosive jumps build the push-off power your turns rely on.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 2.5 min between · strong and powerful (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load + 10 lb per side (women: 2 × 35 lb / men: 2 × 55 lb) · overload over comp
cues: Push the full 50 yards with hard, deliberate power against the heavier sled — this is strength work, not a timed test.
Hold about 45° with arms locked and push through your whole body — don't bend your elbows to absorb the load.
Take long, forceful steps with full hip extension and rest fully so every push is a max-quality effort.

patterns.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps per leg · rest 2 min between · heavy and controlled
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand with your back foot on a bench, heavy enough that the last rep per leg is a strong effort
cues: With your back foot elevated, lower straight down and drive up through your front heel to build single-leg power for the sled.
Keep your torso tall and your front knee tracking over your foot — don't let it cave inward.
Control the descent and pause briefly at the bottom; drop the dumbbells if your balance or form breaks.

patterns.name: Broad Jump
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 reps · rest 2 min between · max-effort explosive reps
patterns.load: Bodyweight only, jumping for distance onto a flat, non-slip surface
cues: Swing your arms and explode forward off both feet, jumping for distance to train the hip drive that powers your push.
Land softly on both feet with bent knees to absorb the landing — don't land stiff-legged.
Stand and reset fully between reps so every jump is fresh, fast, and your hardest effort.
